editmore_vertAnalysis: ChatHub - Your One-Stop LLM ShopKey Technical InsightsLLM Aggregation Platform: ChatHub acts as a centralized interface for accessing and comparing hundreds of large language models (LLMs) including ChatGPT, Llama 2, Claude 2, Mixtral, Gemini, and Perplexity.Multi-Model Simultaneous Inference: The platform's standout feature is the ability to send a single prompt to up to six different models concurrently, allowing for real-time comparison of latency, output length, and creative quality.Cross-Validation Workflow: Users can implement a "mixture of experts" workflow by generating ideas across multiple models and then using a high-tier model (like GPT-4 Turbo) to critique, score, and synthesize the best elements from each.Data Portability (Quick Copy): Includes dedicated UI elements to quickly copy outputs from one model and paste them into another, facilitating multi-step AI pipelines.API and Web App Integration:Web App Mode: Accesses free versions of models (like Claude 2 or Gemini) through web-session wrappers.API Integration: Allows users to input their own API keys (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic) for a "pay-as-you-go" consumption model, bypassing monthly subscriptions like ChatGPT Plus.Poe Integration: Supports models aggregated through the Poe platform.Multimodal Support: Supports image input for models with vision capabilities (currently limited to ChatGPT, Bing, and Gemini) and allows simultaneous image analysis.Prompt Engineering Tools:Prompt Library: A repository of user-created and community prompts (e.g., plagiarism checkers, Excel experts).Prompt Generator: A tool to optimize and expand simple prompts for better LLM performance.UX/UI Innovations: Includes a search function for chat history (a feature notably missing from the native ChatGPT interface) and a "compact view" for better screen real estate management.Business Model Innovation: Unlike most AI services that use SaaS subscriptions, ChatHub uses a "buy once, use forever" lifetime license model for its premium features.🚩 Visual Gaps (For Multimodal Processing)[0:43]: Speaker said "as you can see by this interface". Need to see: The specific layout of the 6-pane comparison window.[1:38]: Speaker said "I've got six llms here... click the send button they all get sent at once". Need to see: The side-by-side streaming of text from Llama 2, Claude 2, Mixtral, etc., to compare generation speed.[2:20]: Speaker said "they have these little quick Copy settings". Need to see: The location and design of the copy/paste shortcut buttons within the UI.[3:11]: Speaker said "giving me some overhead on each individual story I can see that...". Need to see: The table or list generated by GPT-4 Turbo scoring the other models (e.g., the "9.5 out of 10" rating).[3:43]: Speaker said "up top we have the all-in-one interface we can compare either two... three... four and then six". Need to see: The UI toggle/dropdown used to switch between different grid layouts.[4:38]: Speaker said "as you can see by my head down here we've also got a little prompt Library". Need to see: The "Prompt Library" icon and the window displaying the community prompt categories.[6:01]: Speaker said "moving over to the settings... we have a plethora of chat Bots to choose from". Need to see: The settings menu list showing the toggle switches for various models and configuration modes (Web vs. Poe vs. API).[8:27]: Speaker said "as you can see I did set up with an API key through open AI". Need to see: The API configuration screen where users input their secret keys and select specific model versions (like GPT-4-1106-preview).[9:11]: Speaker said "look up the current price of Bitcoin... Claude might not be the best bet... oh hey Bing was also able to pull it up". Need to see: The visual difference between a model with web access (showing search citations) vs. one without.[9:41]: Speaker said "I'll upload a real photo that I took and say critique this image". Need to see: The photo uploaded by the user and the simultaneous "Vision" analysis outputs from Gemini, GPT-4, and Bing.[11:51]: Speaker said "chathub more or less is free and they just have a premium option".
